target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
## Summary

- `resolveGatewayScopedTools` only included `createOpenClawTools` (gateway management tools); `createOpenClawCodingTools` (exec, edit, read, browser, etc.) was never wired in
- The [official docs](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api#tools-invoke-api) describe `exec` as blocked by the HTTP deny list by default — implying the intent was always for coding tools to be available on this surface, gated by `gateway.tools.allow`
- This PR completes that wiring: coding tools are now reachable via `POST /tools/invoke`, subject to the existing deny list and policy pipeline
- Dedup: gateway tools take precedence over coding tools of the same name

## Root Cause

The deny list in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` already included `exec`, `spawn`, `apply_patch`, etc. — designed to gate exactly these tools on the HTTP surface. But `createOpenClawCodingTools` was never passed to `resolveGatewayScopedTools`, so the deny list had nothing to gate: coding tools simply weren't present at all. The docs described the intended behavior; the implementation was incomplete.

## What did NOT change

- Security posture: `exec` and other high-risk tools remain on `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` — blocked by default, require explicit `gateway.tools.allow` to enable
- Agent session runners: no change to how tools are wired for LLM agent runs
- Tool policy pipeline: coding tools pass through the same `applyToolPolicyPipeline` + deny-list as all other tools

## Regression Test Plan

- Added `createOpenClawCodingTools` stub to the `vi.mock("../agents/pi-tools.js")` block in `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — without this, all 22 existing tests failed with 500s (undefined function crash)
- Added new test: `exposes coding tools via /tools/invoke when agent policy allows them` — verifies a coding tool passes through policy + deny-list filtering and is callable via the HTTP surface

```
Tests  24 passed (24)  src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts
```

## Evidence

Production motivation: a LangGraph orchestrator running ruff/bandit/tsc/npm via `/tools/invoke` in an OpenClaw container — eliminates ~240s LLM agent startup overhead per deterministic command call where no model reasoning is needed.

## Human Verification

- `npx vitest run src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — 24/24 pass on a clean branch based on `upstream/main`
- Pre-commit hooks (tsgo, oxlint, lint:auth) passed

## Relationship to existing PRs

- #55906 (`fix(gateway): deny nodes over HTTP tools invoke`) — orthogonal: restricts the surface; this PR expands it. Both coexist.
- #55724 (`fix(gateway): enforce owner-only tools in invoke API`) — orthogonal: enforces owner-only policy. Our change passes `senderIsOwner: true` for HTTP bearer auth, consistent with that direction.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** operator enables `exec` via `gateway.tools.allow` without understanding the RCE surface  
  **Mitigation:** `exec` is on `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` — explicit opt-in required; no behavior change for default installs
- **Risk:** dedup logic (gateway tools shadow coding tools of the same name) could suppress a tool unexpectedly  
  **Mitigation:** gateway and coding tool names are disjoint in practice; merge order is documented in the code comment

Addresses #37131

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): reset progress draft gate started flag on onStart rejection
target.body:
## Summary

`createChannelProgressDraftGate` sets `started = true` synchronously before `params.onStart` resolves. If `onStart` rejects, `started` stays `true` — `noteWork()` returns `true` and `hasStarted` returns `true`, so callers believe streaming is live when it never started. Silent message loss follows (issue F020, #83115).

**Fix:** keep `started = true` synchronously (prevents the double-start race during the async gap), but add a `.catch()` that resets `started` and `startPromise` before re-throwing. Both call sites are covered: the timer path (`void start().catch(() => {})`) swallows the re-throw as before; the `noteWork`/`startNow` path propagates it to the caller.

```diff
-    startPromise = Promise.resolve().then(params.onStart);
+    startPromise = Promise.resolve()
+      .then(params.onStart)
+      .catch((err) => {
+        // onStart rejected — reset so callers don't believe streaming is live
+        started = false;
+        startPromise = undefined;
+        throw err;
+      });
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: after `params.onStart` rejects, `gate.hasStarted` returns `false` and `gate.noteWork()` returns `false`, so retry via `startNow()` becomes possible. Pre-fix both stayed `true` despite never streaming, producing the silent message loss path described in #83115.

Real environment tested: Node 24.14.0 on Linux WSL2 (Ubuntu 24.04); local checkout of `fix/channel-progress-draft-gate-rejection-reset` at `919f90b6df`; vitest 4.1.6 driven by `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
$ git checkout fix/channel-progress-draft-gate-rejection-reset
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts \
    --reporter=verbose \
    src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix (terminal output captured live):

```

```

Observed result after fix: the three new cases above (`resets hasStarted to false when onStart rejects via timer`, `resets hasStarted to false and propagates when onStart rejects via noteWork`, `allows a retry after onStart rejects`) all pass — confirming that after a rejection settles, `gate.hasStarted === false`, the rejection propagates through `startNow()` to the caller, and a follow-up `startNow()` invokes `onStart` again. The other 21 tests confirm no regression in the surrounding gate, label, and progress-line behavior.

What was not tested: end-to-end retry through live channel implementations (Crabbox / Testbox). The fix is a synchronous-flag reset inside the factory; the factory-level behavior is exercised by the unit suite above, but real-channel recovery from a rejecting `onStart` has not been observed against a live channel from this branch.

Closes #83115

target.title: Discord: wake on reaction events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord reaction notifications were enqueued as passive system events, so reaction-only inputs could wait for a later wake before the agent processed them.
- Why it matters: Discord is a low-noise operator surface; a reaction should be enough to get the routed session's attention when reactions are already enabled and authorized.
- What changed: accepted Discord reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and request an immediate heartbeat wake after the system event is successfully enqueued.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no per-emoji semantics/config API, no hardcoded emoji-to-action map, and no new Discord permissions. Emoji meanings can live in preferences/prompt text.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Discord reaction-only events now wake the routed session after enqueue, and standard/custom emoji are exposed as stable reaction keys in the system event text/context key.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch based on `openclaw/main` commit `45a434fb23`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
  - `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: focused Discord monitor Vitest passed `1 file, 46 tests`; extension typecheck passed; format check passed.
- Observed result after fix: standard emoji reactions enqueue `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>`, custom emoji reactions enqueue `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>`, and successfully enqueued reaction events call `requestHeartbeat({ source: "notifications-event", intent: "immediate", reason: "discord-reaction", sessionKey })`.
- What was not tested: I did not send a live Discord reaction through a production bot; maintainers/operators may want redacted live gateway proof before merge.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source inspection showed Discord reaction listeners enqueued system events without a heartbeat wake.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction listener code enqueued system events and returned without requesting a heartbeat wake.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted reaction enqueue/routing behavior but did not assert wake behavior for reaction-only inputs.
- Contributing context (if known): reaction event text included a formatted emoji label but not a stable key that preference text can target consistently.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reaction notifications enqueue stable reaction-key events and wake only when enqueue succeeds.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the Discord monitor harness already covers reaction notification auth, routing, and enqueue behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing reaction tests covered notification enqueue/routing but not heartbeat wake.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls now wake the routed session. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preferences/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without channel-specific semantics config.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Discord reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed only after later wake]

After:
[Discord reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor unit harness
- Relevant config (redacted): default DM reaction test config and guild `reactionNotifications` cases

### Steps

1. Run the focused Discord monitor tests.
2. Run extension typecheck.
3. Run format check for touched files.

### Expected

- Reaction event tests pass, including heartbeat wake assertions.
- Typecheck and format checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: standard emoji enqueue + wake, custom emoji stable key enqueue + wake, no wake when enqueue deduplicates, existing reaction authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked, `off`/`own`/`all`/`allowlist` modes still gate behavior, custom emoji context keys are stable.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord Gateway delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications can now start an agent turn where they previously waited for another wake.
  - Mitigation: behavior is still gated by existing `reactionNotifications` and Discord sender/channel/guild access controls, and duplicate queue entries do not wake.

## Redacted Live Proof

- Generated: 2026-05-17T18:33:53.172Z

- Redacted live proof: Discord API get-current-user succeeded, a canary message was posted to the configured Discord target, a bot reaction was added via Discord REST, and the exact PR-head reaction listener enqueued a system event with a stable reaction_key plus a discord-reaction heartbeat request call. All guild/channel/message/user/session identifiers are SHA-256 short hashes only.
- Live evidence: `{"discordBotIdentity":"verified_with_getCurrentUser","canaryMessagePosted":true,"botReactionAddedViaRest":true,"guildHash":"01738befea7f","channelHash":"5e33a98adfea","messageHash":"9464114d77b7","actorHash":"ab848dd6c34a"}`

target.title: fix(memory-core): use CJK-aware tokenizer for dreaming dedupe (#80613)
target.body:
## Summary

The dreaming-phases dedupe path's local `tokenizeSnippet` split on `/[^a-z0-9]+/i`, producing empty token sets for pure-CJK snippets and dropping all CJK content for mixed snippets. That had two failure modes on current main:

1. Two close paraphrases of the same Chinese fact tokenized to empty sets, fell back to exact-string match, returned similarity `0`, and ended up as duplicate candidates in MEMORY.md.
2. Two semantically distinct CJK snippets that happened to share ASCII tokens (e.g. `Plan` + `exRule`) returned similarity `1.0`, so the dedupe path silently dropped one of the two distinct memories.

The memory MMR layer at `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.ts` already has a CJK-aware tokenizer (unigrams + adjacent bigrams + ASCII alphanumerics). This PR extracts it into `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts` and routes the dreaming dedupe path through the same helper via `textSimilarity`. `mmr.ts` re-exports `tokenize` / `jaccardSimilarity` / `textSimilarity` so existing imports (including `mmr.test.ts`) continue to work without churn.

## Root Cause

- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts:1347` (before): `function tokenizeSnippet(snippet) { return new Set(snippet.toLowerCase().split(/[^a-z0-9]+/i).map(t => t.trim()).filter(Boolean)); }`. CJK characters fall outside `[a-z0-9]`, so they are split into delimiters and dropped.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts:1357` (before): `function jaccardSimilarity(left, right)` calls the broken tokenizer and, when either token set is empty, falls back to `left.trim().toLowerCase() === right.trim().toLowerCase() ? 1 : 0`. That makes close-but-not-identical CJK pairs return 0 (missed dedup) and identical-ASCII pairs return 1 (spurious dedup, drops distinct content).
- Execution path: `dedupeEntries({ entries, threshold })` (`dreaming-phases.ts:1373`) → `jaccardSimilarity(candidate.snippet, entry.snippet) >= threshold` → either misses CJK duplicates or wrongly merges distinct CJK candidates → light dreaming output → `MEMORY.md` accumulates duplicate or loses unique entries.
- clawsweeper's review on #80613 directly endorses this fix shape: *"Keep the issue open and fix the memory-core pipeline with shared CJK-aware tokenization plus a promotion sanitizer that never appends managed dreaming block text to MEMORY.md."* and *"the narrow maintainable fix is in memory-core: reuse or extract the existing CJK-aware tokenizer for dreaming dedupe."*

## Changes

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts` (NEW): extract the CJK-aware `tokenize`, `jaccardSimilarity`, and `textSimilarity` helpers from `mmr.ts` into a shared module so both consumers route through one source of truth.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.ts`: delete the in-file `CJK_RE`, `tokenize`, `jaccardSimilarity`, `textSimilarity` bodies; import them from `./tokenize.js` and re-export verbatim so existing imports (`mmr.test.ts` etc.) keep working.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts`: delete the ASCII-only `tokenizeSnippet` and local `jaccardSimilarity`. Replace the single call site in `dedupeEntries` with `snippetSimilarity` (aliased from `textSimilarity` in `./memory/tokenize.js`). Expose `dedupeEntries` via `__testing` for the regression test.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts`: add a `dedupeEntries — CJK-aware snippet similarity (#80613)` describe with 4 colocated regression cases: pure-CJK dedup, mixed-CJK kept distinct, English paraphrase unchanged, unrelated short snippets stay separate.

Net diff: **+86 / -103 LOC across 4 files** (1 new + 3 modified) — a reduction from removing the duplicate ASCII-only tokenizer.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Dreaming dedupe on CJK content is broken on current main. (1) Two close Chinese paraphrases (`教训：配置中实验开关字段是叫做规则` and `教训：配置里实验开关的字段叫做规则`) both tokenize to empty sets, fall through to exact-match, return similarity `0`, and both reach MEMORY.md instead of one merging into the other. (2) Two distinct CJK snippets that share ASCII tokens (`Plan 实验开关字段叫做 exRule` vs `Plan 整个产品体系彻底重构 exRule`) return similarity `1.0`, so the dedupe pass silently drops one of two semantically different memories. Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80613
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-80613` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The before-fix smoke script reads `dreaming-phases.ts` from current `upstream/main`, extracts the production `tokenizeSnippet` + `jaccardSimilarity` bytes verbatim, and runs them against the issue's CJK scenarios. The after-fix smoke script reads the patched `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts` from this PR head, prints its SHA-256, and runs the shared `tokenize` / `textSimilarity` against the same scenarios. Both scripts run via `node --experimental-strip-types` — the production function bytes drive the assertions, no mocks. PR head: `c497966ee6d12659dc750a1819969d38817f53d1`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/dreaming-cjk-tokenizer && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-verify-80613.mts`, where `smoke-verify-80613.mts` reads the patched `tokenize.ts` from `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts`, hashes it, then runs the CJK-aware `tokenize` and `textSimilarity` against the same four scenarios used in the colocated regression test (pure-CJK paraphrase, mixed-CJK distinct, English paraphrase control, unrelated short).
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `c497966ee6` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/tokenize.ts` exercised via `node --experimental-strip-types` against verbatim source bytes; tokenize.ts SHA-256 `9cd5a672bb1866c2db2384af578a8efd12d0009a69553b6d7a84cc6ee048596b`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-verify-80613.mts
=== Patched tokenize.ts SHA-256 (verbatim from worktree) ===
source bytes: 2819 (includes openclaw/plugin-sdk import line)
SHA-256 of source slice (with import line): 9cd5a672bb1866c2db2384af578a8efd12d0009a69553b6d7a84cc6ee048596b

=== Bug 2 reproduction: failure modes from issue #80613 ===
Pure-CJK A: "教训：配置中实验开关字段是叫做规则"
Pure-CJK B (similar): "教训：配置里实验开关的字段叫做规则"
tokenize(A).size = 30 first 8 tokens: [
  '教训', '配置',
  '置中', '中实',
  '实验', '验开',
  '开关', '关字'
]
tokenize(B).size = 30 first 8 tokens: [
  '教训', '配置',
  '置里', '里实',
  '实验', '验开',
  '开关', '关的'
]
textSimilarity(A, B) = 0.622 (was 0 with ASCII-only tokenizer; threshold 0.5 dedup now succeeds)

Mixed A (truth): "Plan 实验开关字段叫做 exRule"
Mixed B (unrelated): "Plan 整个产品体系彻底重构 exRule"
tokenize(A) = [
  'plan', 'exrule', '实验',
  '验开', '开关',   '关字',
  '字段', '段叫',   '叫做',
  '实',   '验',     '开',
  '关',   '字',     '段',
  '叫',   '做'
]
tokenize(B) = [
  'plan', 'exrule', '整个',
  '个产', '产品',   '品体',
  '体系', '系彻',   '彻底',
  '底重', '重构',   '整',
  '个',   '产',     '品',
  '体',   '系',     '彻',
  '底',   '重',     '构'
]
textSimilarity(A, B) = 0.056 (was 1.0 with ASCII-only tokenizer; threshold 0.7 dedup now correctly keeps both)

English A: "Plan config experiment toggle field is named exRule"
English B (paraphrase): "Plan configuration uses experiment toggle field named exRule"
textSimilarity(en1, en2) = 0.600 (must stay > 0.4 — Latin-script behavior unchanged)

textSimilarity('weather: sunny', 'deploy: blocked') = 0.000 (must stay < 0.3 — no over-collapse)

=== Pass/fail summary ===
[CJK paraphrase dedups]  textSimilarity = 0.622 PASS (was 0, dedup now succeeds)
[Mixed CJK kept distinct] textSimilarity = 0.056 PASS (was 1.0, two distinct facts now kept)
[English paraphrase OK]   textSimilarity = 0.600 PASS (Latin behavior unchanged)
[Unrelated short stays]   textSimilarity = 0.000 PASS (no over-collapse)
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: Pure-CJK paraphrase similarity went from `0.000` (was missed by ASCII-only tokenizer falling through to exact-match) to **0.622** — above the typical `0.5–0.6` dedupe threshold, so the second copy is now correctly merged. The mixed-CJK distinct pair went from `1.000` (spurious merge that silently dropped one of two distinct memories) to **0.056** — well below the `0.7` threshold, so both semantically different snippets are kept. The English paraphrase control stayed at `0.600` (Latin-script behavior unchanged). Unrelated short snippets stayed at `0.000` (no over-collapse). The patched `tokenize.ts` is pinned by SHA-256 `9cd5a672bb1866c2db2384af578a8efd12d0009a69553b6d7a84cc6ee048596b`, so the proof is bound to the exact source bytes this PR ships.
- **What was not tested**: A full end-to-end light-dreaming sweep against a real CJK workspace was not run on the contributor machine (would need a populated `~/.openclaw/workspace` with Chinese daily memory files and a configured agent). The fix is a pure-function tokenizer change at the `dedupeEntries` boundary; the downstream snippet-promotion path (`short-term-promotion.ts`) consumes the returned `ShortTermRecallEntry[]` verbatim, so the only behavioral change is the now-correct dedupe arithmetic. Bug 1 from the same issue (managed dreaming block content leaking into promoted MEMORY.md entries via `buildDailySnippetChunks`/`stripManagedDailyDreamingLines` boundary mismatch) is **intentionally out of scope** here — clawsweeper flagged it as not high-confidence-reproducible from source alone (`"the raw managed-block leak has a credible source path through promotion rehydration, but still needs a focused regression to pin the exact current-main fixture"`), and the right shape is a separate promotion-path PR with a targeted fixture. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live CJK workspace recording is required.

## Test

- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — new `dedupeEntries — CJK-aware snippet similarity (#80613)` describe with 4 cases (CJK paraphrase dedup / mixed-CJK kept distinct / English paraphrase control / unrelated short kept separate); all existing cases continue to pass.
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.test.ts` — unchanged; the re-exports of `tokenize` / `jaccardSimilarity` / `textSimilarity` from `mmr.ts` preserve the existing import surface so this test stays green.
- `pnpm tsgo` against the touched files — clean (LSP diagnostics on `tokenize.ts`, `mmr.ts`, `dreaming-phases.ts`, `dreaming-phases.test.ts` show only pre-existing environment-only `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` resolution warnings; no new TypeScript errors introduced).

## Notes

- Scope: Bug 2 from issue #80613 only (the CJK dedupe path that clawsweeper marked as source-reproducible). Bug 1 (managed-block leak into MEMORY.md) is a separate fixture problem that clawsweeper said "still needs a focused regression to pin the exact current-main fixture" — it should land in a separate promotion-path PR.
- Compatibility: `mmr.ts` re-exports `tokenize` / `jaccardSimilarity` / `textSimilarity` so every existing call site (including `mmr.test.ts`) keeps working without an import-path change. The CJK-aware tokenizer is byte-for-byte the same algorithm `mmr.ts` already shipped, so MMR behavior is unchanged.
- This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #80613: *"the narrow maintainable fix is in memory-core: reuse or extract the existing CJK-aware tokenizer for dreaming dedupe... rather than adding a new config or policy knob."*

Closes #80613

target.title: fix(discord): return subagent thread delivery origin
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord delegated sessions can bind a thread successfully but still post the initial child reply into the parent channel.
- Why it matters: thread-bound delegation looks broken even when the binding exists, weakening isolation for delegated work.
- What changed: the Discord `subagent_spawning` hook now returns a routable Discord `deliveryOrigin` after `autoBindSpawnedDiscordSubagent` succeeds, and the hook test asserts that contract.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no generic spawn-path refactor, no session-store schema change, and no non-Discord behavior change.

- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83170
- Related #42986
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord thread-bound `sessions_spawn` accepted a child session and created the binding, but the initial child reply still landed in the parent channel.
- Real environment tested: local managed OpenClaw runtime on macOS with Discord thread bindings enabled, plus this local source checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts`

- Observed result after fix: the Discord hook returns a routable `deliveryOrigin` for the bound thread in the covered test path.
- What was not tested: full live end-to-end child delivery from this exact source checkout after the patch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): live managed-runtime repro before the patch showed `sessions_spawn(thread=true, mode=session)` accepted while the child still resolved to the parent channel.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.ts` created the thread binding but returned only `threadBindingReady: true`. The generic spawn path only switches the initial child run onto the thread when `subagent_spawning` also returns a routable `deliveryOrigin`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the Discord hook test asserted binding readiness but did not assert the returned delivery target.
- Contributing context (if known): the completion-delivery hook already knew how to derive a bound Discord thread target, but the initial child run path depended on the spawning hook.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient

- Scenario the test should lock in: when Discord `subagent_spawning` successfully binds a thread, it must return `deliveryOrigin` pointing at the bound Discord thread.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failure is in the Discord hook contract, not in generic spawn orchestration.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the existing bind-on-spawn test in the same file, now extended.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

Discord thread-bound delegated child sessions can route their initial reply into the bound thread instead of falling back to the parent channel.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord sessions_spawn(thread=true) -> thread binding created -> initial child reply falls back to parent channel

After:
Discord sessions_spawn(thread=true) -> thread binding created -> deliveryOrigin returned -> initial child reply routes to bound thread
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)

target.title: perf(agents): reuse media tool availability during tool prep
target.body:
## Summary
- Reuse the optional media tool factory plan for the image tool instead of resolving image availability separately.
- Let image/video/music generation tool factories skip their registration-time availability scan when createOpenClawTools already resolved availability.
- Use the same effective media tool config for the plan and constructed media tools.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Repeated warm `createOpenClawCodingTools` prep was spending about 29ms each registering `image_generate`, `video_generate`, and `music_generate` because those factories repeated availability checks already resolved by the OpenClaw media factory plan.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `media-tool-availability-pr4c`, Node 25.9.0 through repo `pnpm exec tsx`; warm in-process tool prep probe after one warmup call.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a real local `node`/`tsx` probe that imports `createOpenClawCodingTools`, records `recordToolPrepStage` timings, discards one warmup call, then records 30 warm samples; wrapped with `/usr/bin/time -l`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
total p50 103.68ms, p95 110.16ms
openclaw-tools:image_generate registration p50 0.005ms
openclaw-tools:video_generate registration p50 0.002ms
openclaw-tools:music_generate registration p50 0.002ms
/usr/bin/time -l max RSS 850575360 bytes
```

Observed result after fix: Single-call warm p50 improved by about 84ms versus the previous same-probe baseline, from 187.74ms p50 to 103.68ms p50 (~45%). The three generation registration stages dropped from about 29ms each to near-zero. Remaining warm prep cost is mostly plugin-tools (~73ms p50) and image/media availability planning (~30ms p50).

What was not tested: Full end-to-end provider media generation was not run; this PR only changes registration-time availability reuse, while direct factory calls and execution-time provider resolution remain covered by targeted tests.

## Verification
- pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.media-factory-plan.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core
- pnpm build
- pnpm check:changed --staged

target.title: Discord: wake on reaction events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord reaction notifications were enqueued as passive system events, so reaction-only inputs could wait for a later wake before the agent processed them.
- Why it matters: Discord is a low-noise operator surface; a reaction should be enough to get the routed session's attention when reactions are already enabled and authorized.
- What changed: accepted Discord reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and request an immediate heartbeat wake after the system event is successfully enqueued.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no per-emoji semantics/config API, no hardcoded emoji-to-action map, and no new Discord permissions. Emoji meanings can live in preferences/prompt text.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Discord reaction-only events now wake the routed session after enqueue, and standard/custom emoji are exposed as stable reaction keys in the system event text/context key.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch based on `openclaw/main` commit `45a434fb23`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
  - `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: focused Discord monitor Vitest passed `1 file, 46 tests`; extension typecheck passed; format check passed.
- Observed result after fix: standard emoji reactions enqueue `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>`, custom emoji reactions enqueue `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>`, and successfully enqueued reaction events call `requestHeartbeat({ source: "notifications-event", intent: "immediate", reason: "discord-reaction", sessionKey })`.
- What was not tested: I did not send a live Discord reaction through a production bot; maintainers/operators may want redacted live gateway proof before merge.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source inspection showed Discord reaction listeners enqueued system events without a heartbeat wake.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction listener code enqueued system events and returned without requesting a heartbeat wake.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted reaction enqueue/routing behavior but did not assert wake behavior for reaction-only inputs.
- Contributing context (if known): reaction event text included a formatted emoji label but not a stable key that preference text can target consistently.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reaction notifications enqueue stable reaction-key events and wake only when enqueue succeeds.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the Discord monitor harness already covers reaction notification auth, routing, and enqueue behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing reaction tests covered notification enqueue/routing but not heartbeat wake.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls now wake the routed session. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preferences/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without channel-specific semantics config.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Discord reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed only after later wake]

After:
[Discord reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor unit harness
- Relevant config (redacted): default DM reaction test config and guild `reactionNotifications` cases

### Steps

1. Run the focused Discord monitor tests.
2. Run extension typecheck.
3. Run format check for touched files.

### Expected

- Reaction event tests pass, including heartbeat wake assertions.
- Typecheck and format checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: standard emoji enqueue + wake, custom emoji stable key enqueue + wake, no wake when enqueue deduplicates, existing reaction authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked, `off`/`own`/`all`/`allowlist` modes still gate behavior, custom emoji context keys are stable.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord Gateway delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications can now start an agent turn where they previously waited for another wake.
  - Mitigation: behavior is still gated by existing `reactionNotifications` and Discord sender/channel/guild access controls, and duplicate queue entries do not wake.

## Redacted Live Proof

- Canary hash: ff1008fe6ee9
- Proof status: pass
- Redacted live proof: Discord API get-current-user succeeded, a canary message was posted to the configured Discord target, a bot reaction was added via Discord REST, and the exact PR-head reaction listener enqueued a system event with a stable reaction_key plus a discord-reaction heartbeat request call. All guild/channel/message/user/session identifiers are SHA-256 short hashes only.
- Live evidence: `{"discordBotIdentity":"verified_with_getCurrentUser","canaryMessagePosted":true,"botReactionAddedViaRest":true,"guildHash":"01738befea7f","channelHash":"5e33a98adfea","messageHash":"9464114d77b7","actorHash":"ab848dd6c34a"}`

target.title: fix(tailscale): retry status --json after transient startup failures
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #42798

After `enableTailscaleServe`, `getTailnetHostname` can run `tailscale status --json` while the CLI is still settling and log a scary transient failure. This adds `readTailscaleStatusJson()` with **3 attempts** and **500ms** backoff (same path used by `getTailnetHostname`).

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/tailscale.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/tailscale-status-json-retry-live-proof.mjs`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Transient `Command failed: tailscale status --json` during gateway Tailscale serve startup when status is queried immediately after serve enable.

**Real environment tested:** macOS, Node 22, local checkout at this branch (injected exec simulating first-call failure).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/tailscale-status-json-retry-live-proof.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
status calls: 2
host: proof-host.tailnet.ts.net
```

**Observed result after fix:** First simulated `status --json` failure is retried; hostname resolves on the second call instead of aborting.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway restart with Homebrew Tailscale daemon under load; funnel mode status paths (unchanged).

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: fix(agents): preserve run-mode keep subagents past session sweep TTL (#83132)
target.body:
## Summary

Refs #83132. Companion to #83146.

The sweeper in `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` applies `SESSION_RUN_TTL_MS` (5 minutes after `cleanupCompletedAt`) to any registry entry with `archiveAtMs === undefined`. The comment claims this is for session-mode runs, but `archiveAtMs` is **also** undefined by design for **run-mode** subagents spawned with `cleanup: "keep"` (`subagent-registry-run-manager.ts:475-480`):

```ts
const archiveAtMs =
  spawnMode === "session" || registerParams.cleanup === "keep"
    ? undefined
    : archiveAfterMs
      ? now + archiveAfterMs
      : undefined;
```

Result: a `cleanup: "keep"` run-mode subagent finishes → `cleanupCompletedAt` set → five minutes later the sweeper deletes it from `subagents/runs.json` while sessions/transcripts stay on disk. This matches the symptom in #83132 — *"five accepted native subagent children whose sessions were present in the session store, but none of the five run IDs appeared in `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json`"* — the reporter explicitly used `runtime:"subagent"`, `mode:"run"`, `cleanup:"keep"`.

Fix: gate the absolute-TTL prune so it does not apply to non-session `cleanup: "keep"` entries.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Issue #83132 — `cleanup: "keep"` run-mode subagents disappear from `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json` 5 minutes after cleanup completes, despite `keep` meaning "retain". After this patch they stay registered.

- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on `/private/tmp/openclaw`. macOS 25.3.0 (Darwin arm64). `node v25.9.0`, `pnpm 11.1.0`. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` clean. Live demo loads the real `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` via `tsx` and runs the real sweeper against a real `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json` on a fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` tempdir — no vitest, no fake timers, no mocked persistence. Only the external gateway/announce dependencies are stubbed because there is no live agent to call.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `git checkout fix/subagent-sweeper-keep-mode-ttl-83132`
  2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
  3. Drop a local-only repro file at `scripts/repro-83132-sweep.mjs` (not committed) that:
     - sets `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` to a fresh tempdir,
     - imports `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` via tsx,
     - registers a run-mode `cleanup: "keep"` entry whose `cleanupCompletedAt` is back-dated 10 minutes (twice the `SESSION_RUN_TTL_MS = 5 min` cutoff),
     - calls `__testing.sweepOnceForTests()` against the real sweeper,
     - reads back the in-memory list and the on-disk `runs.json`.
  4. `node --import tsx scripts/repro-83132-sweep.mjs`
  5. `git checkout main` and repeat step 4 against unmodified main to capture the pre-fix behavior.

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from both invocations, captured from my local shell on `/private/tmp/openclaw`. The script writes the entry to disk via `saveSubagentRegistryToDisk` before sweeping, so the after-sweep disk check is a real persisted-registry observation. Stdout reproduced verbatim.

  **After patch (`fix/subagent-sweeper-keep-mode-ttl-83132`):**
  ```
  $ node --import tsx scripts/repro-83132-sweep.mjs
  --- before sweep ---
  registry: live-run-keep-run-mode
  disk /var/folders/.../openclaw-sweep-repro-i16CDM/subagents/runs.json:
  {
    "version": 2,
    "runs": {
      "live-run-keep-run-mode": {
        "runId": "live-run-keep-run-mode",
        "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:live-keep",
        "requesterSessionKey": "agent:main:main",
        "requesterDisplayKey": "main",
        "task": "live keep-mode run-mode subagent",
        "cleanup": "keep",
        "spawnMode": "run",
        "createdAt": 1779034336836,
        "startedAt": 1779034336836,
        "endedAt": 1779034337836,
        "cleanupHandled": true,
        "cleanupCompletedAt": 1779034337836,
        "outcome": { "status": "ok", "startedAt": 1779034336836, "endedAt": 1779034337836, "elapsedMs": 1000 }
      }
    }
  }
  cleanupCompletedAt age: 10.00 min (SESSION_RUN_TTL_MS = 5 min)
  --- after sweep ---
  registry: live-run-keep-run-mode
  disk /var/folders/.../openclaw-sweep-repro-i16CDM/subagents/runs.json:
  {
    "version": 2,
    "runs": {
      "live-run-keep-run-mode": {
        "runId": "live-run-keep-run-mode",
        "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:live-keep",
        ...
        "cleanupCompletedAt": 1779034337836,
        "outcome": { "status": "ok", ... }
      }
    }
  }
  RESULT: PASS — entry survived sweep both in-memory and on-disk
  ```

  **Before patch (`main` at `833f1ce7`), same script, same setup:**
  ```
  $ node --import tsx scripts/repro-83132-sweep.mjs
  --- before sweep ---
  registry: live-run-keep-run-mode
  disk /var/folders/.../openclaw-sweep-repro-hZiHkV/subagents/runs.json:
  {
    "version": 2,
    "runs": {
      "live-run-keep-run-mode": { ... "cleanup": "keep", "spawnMode": "run", "cleanupCompletedAt": 1779034347260, ... }
    }
  }
  cleanupCompletedAt age: 10.00 min (SESSION_RUN_TTL_MS = 5 min)
  --- after sweep ---
  registry: (none)
  disk /var/folders/.../openclaw-sweep-repro-hZiHkV/subagents/runs.json:
  {
    "version": 2,
    "runs": {}
  }

  RESULT: FAIL — entry pruned from both in-memory and on-disk
  ```

  The post-sweep on-disk `runs.json` `{ "version": 2, "runs": {} }` on `main` is exactly the symptom in the bug report: *"none of the five run IDs appeared in `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json`"*. After the patch, the sweeper leaves both the in-memory entry and the on-disk row untouched.

- **Observed result after fix:** With the patch, `cleanup: "keep"` + `spawnMode: "run"` entries survive the real sweeper past 10 minutes (twice the prior TTL window). Both the in-memory `listSubagentRunsForRequester` result and the persisted `~/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json` file still contain the run row. Pre-fix, the same setup leaves an empty `runs.json` on disk and the list returns nothing for that runId. Session-mode (`spawnMode: "session"`) runs still get pruned 5 minutes after cleanup — the gate only exempts the non-session keep path.

- **What was not tested:** I did not exercise the original live QQ / Discord embedded-channel five-children wave from the bug report (no local QQ harness). I also did not run the full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` end-to-end locally; CI on this PR re-runs that.

### Supplemental regression coverage (mocks, not the proof)

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

New regression case in `subagent-registry.test.ts` ("preserves run-mode keep entries past SESSION_RUN_TTL_MS sweep") fails on `main`, passes on this branch.

## Why not in #83146

#83146 lands the narrow "fail-closed initial registration" durability fix Clawsweeper recommended. This PR is a separate, smaller surface (one-line gate on the sweeper + one regression test) that targets the actual root-cause path for the reporter's exact `cleanup:"keep"` scenario. Keeping it isolated so each fix can be reviewed and reverted independently.

## Test plan

- [x] `node --import tsx scripts/repro-83132-sweep.mjs` on the fix branch (local repro file, not committed) — see Evidence above
- [x] Same script on `main` to capture pre-fix prune behavior — see Evidence above
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
- [ ] CI: full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` lane on this PR

target.body:
## Summary

- **Motivation (dated, citable).** On **May 13, 2026** Anthropic announced that, starting **June 15, 2026**, Claude subscriptions split into two pools: **interactive usage stays on existing subscription limits** (Claude Code, Cowork, claude.ai), while **programmatic usage moves onto a separate fixed monthly credit pool** — explicitly enumerated as Agent SDK, `claude -p` (the non-interactive command), Claude Code GitHub Actions, **and third-party apps that auth with the subscription through the Agent SDK** (i.e. OpenClaw on the existing `claude-cli` backend). The programmatic credit is fixed per tier (Pro $20 / Max 5x $100 / Max 20x $200 / Team $100/seat / Enterprise $200/seat), billed at API rates, and does **not** roll over. Coverage: [VentureBeat — "Anthropic reinstates OpenClaw and third-party agent usage on Claude subscriptions — with a catch"](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch), [SiliconANGLE — "Anthropic announces ‘programmatic credit pool’ as agentic tool use rises"](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/14/anthropic-announces-programmatic-credit-pool-agentic-tool-use-rises/).
- **Why this PR exists.** OpenClaw's existing `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` — i.e. exactly the non-interactive command the announcement names as moving to the programmatic credit pool on June 15. After June 15 every OpenClaw turn on a Claude subscription will drain the fixed credit pool at API rates rather than count against the user's interactive subscription limits, and Opus turns in particular will exhaust the credit very quickly. This PR adds a second backend that drives `claude` in interactive mode (no `-p`) so OpenClaw stays on the interactive pool that subscribers are actually paying for.
- **What changed.** A new `claude-cli-interactive` backend runs `claude` without `-p` (interactive mode) under a per-turn `bun` wrapper that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM proxy, taps every API SSE event to reconstruct `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records on its own stdout, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload tricks claude into staying in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** The existing `claude-cli` backend is untouched (its predicate `liveSession === "claude-stdio"` matches it alone now); user `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` overrides flow through to the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with only `-p`-only flags stripped; the system CA store is **not** mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` is scoped to the spawned claude child only); non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) pass through unmodified; tool-use / timestamp / "waiting on tool" injection into the reasoning stream is intentionally **out of scope** here and lands as a separate follow-up to #81550 that rides on the `stream_event` JSONL surface this PR establishes.
- AI-assisted: yes — drafted with Claude.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR introduces the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend; the follow-up tool-use injection PR will work on both)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression
- [x] This PR adds a new backend behind an opt-in backend id

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `claude` running under OpenClaw in **interactive (subscription) mode** with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- Real environment tested: Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm install), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- Exact steps run after this patch:
  - `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm test:extension anthropic` — focused vitest run of the anthropic extension test files.
  - Set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` as the active backend in user config, restart the OpenClaw gateway, send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn, observe the wrapper boot in stderr, the API SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>` and arrive at OpenClaw as `stream_event` records.
  - Send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` (default sessionMode) and verify the second turn picks up the existing claude session.
  - With `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` set, verify stderr includes `[openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: …\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe` (the npm-installed claude, not a winget-shadowed install).
- Evidence after fix:
  - `pnpm test:extension anthropic` → `Test Files 7 passed (7) / Tests 73 passed (73) / Duration 10.41s`. New `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` covers the three normalizeConfig branches (override path → `command="bun"` + env passthrough; preserves other env entries; **no override → env passthrough omitted** so wrapper.ts can fall back to a PATH lookup of `claude`).
  - Live Telegram turn: per-turn process emits `init` → `stream_event*` → single `result` on `end_turn`, all sourced from the proxy's SSE tap.
- Observed result after fix: per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, then a continuous stream of `stream_event` records (tapped from the proxy), then a single `result` record on `end_turn`, then sets `intentionalKill = true` and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits with code 0.
- **Live capture from the running install** — redacted artifacts on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch of my fork:
  - [`.proof/gateway-log-excerpt.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/gateway-log-excerpt.txt) — 30 lines from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` covering three real per-turn invocations: gateway logs `cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive` + `cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose …`, wrapper stderr logs `certs ready` → `MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081` → `spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe` → `result flushed, waiting for session write before kill` → `session file updated, killing claude`. Verbatim, just paths/session-ids redacted.
  - [`.proof/proxy-certs-listing.txt`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/proxy-certs-listing.txt) — `~/.openclaw/proxy-certs/` directory listing + `openssl x509 -noout` output for both certs. CA: `CN=OpenClaw Proxy CA`, self-signed, valid `May 14 22:30:14 2026 GMT` → `May 14 22:30:14 2027 GMT`, SHA1 `63:69:13:08:D9:AE:CC:CC:85:D3:A7:B2:AB:E0:A6:06:E3:88:97:2E`. Leaf: `CN=api.anthropic.com`, issued by the CA, SAN `DNS:api.anthropic.com, DNS:statsig.anthropic.com, DNS:console.anthropic.com`.
  - [`.proof/openclaw-config-excerpt.json`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof/openclaw-config-excerpt.json) — `agents.defaults.model.primary` is `claude-cli-interactive/claude-opus-4-6`; `agents.defaults.models` registers `claude-cli-interactive` runtimes for opus-4-7/4-6/4-5, sonnet-4-6/4-5, haiku-4-5. Production wiring, not a test fixture.

  Embedded sample (one of the three turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

  ```
  cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus promptChars=N trigger=user useResume=true session=present resumeSession=<SESSION> reuse=reusable historyPrompt=present
  cli argv: bun <NPM>\node_modules\openclaw\dist\extensions\anthropic\interactive-proxy\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --permission-mode bypassPermissions --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <TMP>\openclaw-cli-mcp-<TMP>\mcp.json --model opus <prompt:N chars>
  [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
  [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 -- Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
  [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
  [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill, path: <HOME>\.claude\projects\<WORKSPACE>\<SESSION>.jsonl
  [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
  [openclaw-proxy] claude exited, code:  signal: SIGTERM intentional: true
  ```
- What was not tested:
  - Linux/macOS (developed and exercised on Windows 11 only); the wrapper has no Windows-specific code beyond the binary-resolution guard, but I have not run it on Linux/macOS.
  - Bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only the `mcp__openclaw__*` allowlist path was exercised live).
  - Behaviour against an Anthropic-side `429`/`overloaded_error` mid-stream (the wrapper passes the SSE stream through unchanged, so the existing claude error-handling should apply, but I did not force the failure to confirm).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — this is a feature PR, not a bugfix. The condition this addresses (no thinking visibility on subscription) is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` — asserts `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape (forces `command="bun"`, stashes inherited binary on env when an override exists, omits the env when no override exists).
- Scenario the test should lock in: the post-merge normalize step survives both shapes the runtime can produce — (a) user has a `claude-cli` override → `merged.command` is the inherited absolute path, env passthrough must be set; (b) user has no override → `merged.command` stays as the backend's own `"bun"`, env passthrough must be **omitted** so wrapper.ts doesn't try to spawn bun as the claude binary.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig`, fully exercised by calling `backend.normalizeConfig(merged)` synchronously and asserting the output keys. An e2e run would also catch it but cost minutes per run; the unit test covers all three branches in milliseconds and runs in CI on every PR.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A — the file is new with this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — three new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New backend id: `claude-cli-interactive`. Selectable via `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` user config; nothing changes for users on `claude-cli`.
- When the new backend is active, every claude turn spawns a fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` process that boots a loopback MITM proxy on `127.0.0.1:<port>` for the lifetime of that turn. Process is killed on `end_turn`; loopback ports are released.
- Reasoning stream now carries plaintext `thinking_delta` content on the subscription path (previously visible only on the API-key billing path).
- Title-generation auxiliary turns (claude's side request whose only text content is `{"title":"…"}`) are buffered and silently suppressed before the wrapper emits any records to OpenClaw.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
OpenClaw gateway
  │  spawns one fresh wrapper per turn:
  ▼
bun wrapper.ts [--session-id …] [--resume …] [prompt-as-positional-arg]
  ├─ cert-manager.ts → generate/cache local CA + api.anthropic.com leaf cert
  ├─ mitm-server.ts  → Stage 1: HTTP CONNECT proxy (loopback only)
  │                    Stage 2: Bun TLS server (terminates TLS, re-issues upstream)
  │                    onEvent(evt) tap on every parsed SSE event
  ▼ NODE_OPTIONS="--require <abs>/tty-spoof.cjs"
    HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>
    NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=<ca.crt>   ← scoped to this claude child only
  ▼
claude.exe (interactive mode, no -p, ink-based TUI rendering)
  │  API SSE → CONNECT → TLS terminate → tap event → forward to claude
  │  TUI stdout → /dev/null (we ignore it)
  ▼
wrapper.ts emits init / stream_event* / result JSONL on its own stdout,
sourced entirely from the tapped SSE — output identical to the shape
`claude -p --output-format stream-json` produces.
```

### Per-turn kill/restart lifecycle (why we don't use the persistent live-session model)

`claude-cli-interactive` does **not** use the persistent `claude-stdio` live-session model that `claude-cli` uses. Each turn = one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` invocation:

1. Wrapper boots, lazily generates/loads the local CA, starts the MITM proxy on a loopback port.
2. Wrapper emits `{"type":"init","session_id":"…"}` on its stdout.
3. Wrapper spawns claude over a piped stdin; framework stdin is forwarded to `claude.stdin` so OpenClaw's prompt-as-stdin convention still works.
4. SSE events flow through the proxy → wrapper's `onEvent` handler → wrapper emits `{"type":"stream_event","event":…}` JSONL per event.
5. On `message_stop` with `stop_reason === "end_turn"`, wrapper assembles `{"type":"result","session_id":"…","result":"…","is_error":false,"usage":{…}}`, drains it to stdout, sets `intentionalKill = true`, then `claude.kill()`s the child.
6. Wrapper exits 0; loopback ports released.

Why kill on `end_turn` instead of letting claude self-exit: interactive claude doesn't exit after one turn — it's a TUI awaiting further input. The wrapper has to be authoritative about turn boundaries from the SSE side. `intentionalKill` distinguishes this from claude crashing.

### TTY spoof (`interactive-proxy/tty-spoof.cjs`)

Claude refuses interactive (subscription) mode unless stdin/stdout look like a TTY: it falls back to headless mode the moment `process.stdin.isTTY` is undefined or `tty.isatty(0)` returns false. Under OpenClaw it's spawned with piped stdio, so neither is true. `tty-spoof.cjs` is preloaded into the claude child via `NODE_OPTIONS=--require <abs-path>` and patches:

- `process.stdin.isTTY`, `process.stdout.isTTY`, `process.stderr.isTTY` → `true` (property level).
- `tty.isatty(fd)` → returns `true` for fd 0/1/2 (the ink renderer calls this directly via libuv, bypassing the property).
- `process.stdin.setRawMode` → no-op stub (ink calls this; would throw on a pipe).
- `process.stdout.columns`/`rows` and `process.stderr.columns`/`rows` → defaulted to 200×50 so ink layout has dimensions to render against.

Minimum surface needed for claude to enter and stay in interactive mode under piped stdio. Nothing else is patched — claude still does its real work, makes real API calls, and emits its real TUI bytes (we discard them).

### Full JSONL reconstruction (backwards compatibility with `claude -p --output-format stream-json`)

OpenClaw's JSONL parser expects exactly the output shape of `claude -p --output-format stream-json`. The wrapper reproduces it record-for-record, sourced entirely from the proxy:

- **`init`** (session_id) — emitted on wrapper boot, before claude even spawns.
- **`stream_event`** (one per tapped SSE event) — forwarded verbatim from the proxy: `message_start`, `content_block_start`, `content_block_delta` (`text_delta` / `thinking_delta` / `signature_delta` / `tool_use`), `content_block_stop`, `message_delta`, `message_stop`, `ping`. The proxy emits everything; the wrapper emits everything; OpenClaw's JSONL parser sees the same shape it would have on the `-p` path.
- **`result`** (one per turn) — synthesised on `end_turn` from accumulated text-delta concatenation + final usage from `message_delta`.

The proxy is a thin pass-through — it tees every parsed SSE event to a single `onEvent` handler and otherwise forwards the SSE stream unmodified to claude. No event-specific logic lives in the proxy; the wrapper interprets everything. To avoid wasted CPU on auxiliary turns the proxy only taps SSE responses for requests that contain a non-empty `tools` array (real conversation turns); title-gen and other no-tool requests pass through without inspection.

### Binary resolution

`normalizeConfig` runs after `mergeBackendConfig` shallow-spreads the inherited `claude-cli` override on top of this backend's base config. The merge replaces `command` with the inherited absolute claude binary path. The normalize step:

- Forces `command` back to `"bun"` so the wrapper actually runs as a script (otherwise `claude.exe` would be spawned with `wrapper.ts` as a positional and the proxy would never start).
- Stashes the inherited binary path on `env.OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` so `wrapper.ts` spawns that exact binary instead of doing a PATH lookup. PATH lookups on Windows resolve via PATHEXT to whichever `claude.exe` arrived first — a winget install can shadow the npm install OpenClaw configured.
- **Guards the no-override case**: if `merged.command` is still our backend's own `"bun"` (no `claude-cli` override took effect), the env passthrough is **omitted** so `wrapper.ts` falls back to a PATH lookup of `"claude"` rather than trying to spawn bun as the claude binary.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **Yes** — a per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child only via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store is not modified. Local CA + leaf private key are generated lazily on first use and cached under the user's OpenClaw config dir. The CA exists solely to issue a single `api.anthropic.com` leaf cert that the per-turn child trusts via the env var; no other process consumes it.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** — the wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token. It is forwarded transparently in the request that the proxy re-issues upstream over real TLS.
- New/changed network calls? **Yes** — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. The proxy CONNECTs to the real upstream and reissues each request unmodified; the request body is read once to detect the `tools` field for the SSE-tap decision and is forwarded byte-for-byte. Non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools set (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same `--setting-sources user`, same `--session-id` semantics.
- Data access scope changed? **No** — the proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing is exfiltrated, persisted, or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

Risk + mitigation summary for the Yes items: the trust boundary changes for Anthropic traffic of *this child process only*. Mitigation: child-scoped `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, no system CA mutation, CA private key restricted to the user's OpenClaw config dir, no other process configured to trust it. Backend is opt-in by id (`claude-cli-interactive`), not a default. The whole reason for this surface is to keep OpenClaw on the interactive subscription pool after the June 15 split — the `-p` (programmatic) path that doesn't intercept TLS remains the default, and users keep that experience by selecting the existing `claude-cli` backend.

## Prior art / alternatives considered

- [`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) takes a different approach to keeping Claude on the interactive subscription pool: it runs `claude` without `-p` and bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely. That works for the raw final text, but **loses the JSONL `stream_event` records that any reasoning stream or tool-use rendering depends on**. The JSONL-reconstruction approach in this PR (re-emitting the same record shape `claude -p --output-format stream-json` produces, sourced from the SSE tap) preserves the existing OpenClaw stream consumer unchanged — so the existing reasoning bridge and the tool-use surface in the follow-up PR layer on cleanly without losing fidelity.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** — the existing `claude-cli` backend is unchanged. Adding the new backend is opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- Config/env changes? **No** — no new required user config. New optional env var `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose stderr from the wrapper.
- Migration needed? **No**.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a newly-installed `claude-cli` user with no inherited override could end up spawning `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` blindly stores `merged.command` in the env passthrough.
  - Mitigation: `normalizeConfig` skips the env passthrough when `merged.command === "bun"`; the wrapper falls back to a PATH lookup of `"claude"`. Regression test in `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` asserts the env var is `undefined` in that branch.
- Risk: the `tty-spoof.cjs` preload is a runtime support file, not a build output, so it could be omitted from packaged installs and break interactive mode at runtime with `MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.
  - Mitigation: the file is declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets` so the postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper, mitm-server, and cert-manager.
- Risk: a winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install on Windows because `spawn("claude")` resolves via PATHEXT, leading to `unknown option '--effort'` on older claude versions.
  - Mitigation: when an override is present, `wrapper.ts` spawns the exact binary the parent backend resolved (passed via `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY`) instead of falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- Risk: title-generation side requests can land mid-turn and inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - Mitigation: the wrapper buffers early `text_delta`s when the accumulated text starts with `{`; if it parses as JSON with a `title` field the entire turn is suppressed before any record is emitted to OpenClaw. Defence-in-depth: the proxy already skips SSE tapping for requests without a `tools` array, so most auxiliary requests never reach the wrapper at all.
- Risk: TLS interception persists a local CA + leaf key under the user's OpenClaw config dir indefinitely; an attacker with read access to that directory could craft a leaf cert trusted by the per-turn child.
  - Mitigation: trust is scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` (no other process opts in), the system trust store is unchanged, and the CA exists solely to issue the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs. Maintainer security review explicitly invited as part of this PR.

target.title: fix(doctor): detect stale session snapshot paths
target.body:
Fixes #47534.

## Summary
- add a read-only doctor health contribution for stale cached session snapshot metadata
- scan persisted session stores for cached bundled skill paths in `skillsSnapshot.prompt`, runtime-only `skillsSnapshot.resolvedSkills`, and `systemPromptReport.injectedWorkspaceFiles`
- distinguish healthy live bundled skills root from stale cached metadata paths and avoid false positives for current runtime paths, workspace skill roots, and Windows paths

## Tests
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-transcripts.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor` did not warn when cached session snapshot metadata still referenced bundled skills under an inactive OpenClaw runtime root after an install/runtime path change.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu source checkout with deterministic temp session-store fixtures and the real doctor session snapshot scanner registered through the doctor health contribution list.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-transcripts.test.ts`, `pnpm check:changed`, and the scripted before/after proof published below.

Evidence after fix: ![before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-82867-fresh/pr-82867/pr-82867-doctor-session-snapshots-before-after-proof.png)

- Summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-82867-fresh/pr-82867/summary.txt
- Before log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-82867-fresh/pr-82867/before-origin-main.log
- After log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-82867-fresh/pr-82867/after-pr-82867.log

Observed result after fix: Before `origin/main`, no doctor session snapshot detector/contribution exists. After this PR, stale POSIX install-root, temp-backed, and Windows cached bundled skill paths are reported, while current-runtime and workspace skill paths are not false positives. The doctor note distinguishes the healthy live bundled skills root from stale cached session metadata and gives safe fresh-session/reset guidance.

What was not tested: A live macOS Homebrew install migration was not run; the proof uses source-level session-store fixtures for the stale cached metadata shape from the issue.

## Summary

- Problem: Gateway startup secret resolution and web-tool credential selection were hard to inspect without exposing raw secret values.
- Why it matters: startup regressions around env, file, exec, auth profile, and web provider secrets need traceable timings and sanitized diagnostics.
- What changed: add runtime secrets diagnostics spans, sanitized provider/ref attributes, startup trace output, web-tool diagnostics, and benchmark coverage for secret-backed startup cases.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no new secret provider capability, no credential value logging, no user config migration, and no provider selection policy change.
- AI-assisted: Yes; Codex assisted with implementation, review, rebase conflict resolution, and local verification.
